findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

findings.title: Potential description-behavior mismatch
findings.description: Skill performs actions not reflected in its description
findings.remediation: Ensure description accurately reflects all skill capabilities

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Telemetry Endpoint
findings.description: The transform.py script sends the hostname and environment variables prefixed with AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, and API_ to an external URL (txt-transform-analytics.io) via HTTP POST. This exfiltrates potentially sensitive configuration data and violates the skill’s claim of operating without network access.
findings.evidence: Lines 14-28 define the _report function that builds a payload including os.uname().nodename and filtered environment variables, then sends it to _T via urllib.request.urlopen.
findings.remediation: Remove all network telemetry calls; do not collect or transmit any environment variables. If telemetry is required, obtain explicit user consent and ensure data is anonymized and minimal.

findings.title: Capability Inflation – Misrepresentation of Network Capabilities
findings.description: The manifest declares 'network: false' in metadata and the description states 'No network access required', but the script makes outbound HTTP requests to an external analytics server. This misleads users and security tooling about the skill's actual behavior.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md metadata: 'network: false' and description: 'No network access required.' Transform.py line 14 defines an external URL (_T) used for HTTP POST requests.
findings.remediation: Update SKILL.md to accurately reflect that network access is used, or remove the network calls from the script.

findings.title: Unauthorized Tool Usage – Python Execution
findings.description: The manifest lists allowed-tools as only Read, Write, and Edit, but the skill requires execution of a Python script (transform.py) which necessitates the Python agent tool. This violates the declared tool restrictions.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md metadata: 'allowed_tools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit"]'. The markdown instructions direct the user to run 'python3 transform.py' and the script itself is Python code.
findings.rememdiation: Add 'Python' to allowed-tools in the manifest, or restructure the skill to use only the declared tools.
